description: Multi-agent crew - researcher gathers, fact-verifier challenges, analyst synthesizes into executive brief with peer review and one corrected error

payload.input: Brief me on the EV battery cell-to-pack (CTP) and cell-to-chassis (CTC) market. I need: top 5 manufacturers by 2025 volume share, current pack-level cost trajectory, where structural-battery technology differentiation is heading for 2026-2028, and any geopolitical supply concentration risk. Output as a 200-word executive brief suitable for a board pre-read.

payload.purpose: comprehensive market research using web_search and financial_data_api tools across 4 sub-questions

payload.output:
Research complete across 4 sub-questions, 18 sources consulted (BloombergNEF, S&P Global Mobility, IEA, Reuters, company filings).

Manufacturer share 2025 (CTP/CTC pack volumes for EV passenger vehicles): CATL 35.2%, BYD 16.8%, LG Energy Solution 13.1%, Panasonic 6.4%, Samsung SDI 4.9%. Note CATL share includes Qilin platform; BYD share reflects vertical integration with own EVs (not merchant sales).

Cost: pack-level $115/kWh (2022) -> $98/kWh (2023) -> $89/kWh (2024) -> $82/kWh (2025), tracking $70-72/kWh by 2027 per BNEF Lithium-Ion Battery Price Survey 2025. The $100/kWh barrier breached 2024.

Structural battery: Tesla 4680 + Cybertruck shipping; BYD Seal e-Platform 3.0 in production; Leapmotor B10 launched late 2025. Direction: cell-to-chassis (CTC) eliminates pack housing, 30-40% parts reduction, but ties customer to single-vendor stack and complicates collision repair (pack cannot be replaced as unit).

Geopolitical: 2025 China-origin share of CTP/CTC supply ~78% (CATL+BYD+CALB+EVE). U.S. IRA Section 30D tightens 'foreign entity of concern' rules for 2025+, reducing eligibility for Chinese-content packs. EU Critical Raw Materials Act (eff 2024) targets 65% from any single non-EU country by 2030.

payload.reason: Research complete with specific numeric claims (volume share, cost figures, regulatory dates) that must be verified before being put in front of the board - peer review is mandatory in this crew

payload.purpose: independent verification of 4 numeric claims against primary sources

payload.output:
Verification complete. THREE claims verified, ONE correction needed.

(1) CATL 2025 share 35.2% - VERIFIED against SNE Research October 2025 monthly report (35.1%) and BNEF Q3 2025 (35.4%) - the 35.2% figure is within the source range, accept.

(2) Pack-level $82/kWh 2025 - VERIFIED against BNEF Lithium-Ion Battery Price Survey 2025 (volume-weighted average $82/kWh) and Goldman Sachs December 2025 estimate ($83/kWh).

(3) CTC parts reduction 30-40% - VERIFIED in concept but the specific 30-40% range comes from Tesla Battery Day 2020 and BYD whitepaper - it is the manufacturer's own claim, not independent measurement. Recommend qualifying as 'manufacturer-claimed' rather than asserting as fact.

(4) China-origin CTP/CTC supply 78% - INCORRECT. Researcher conflated cell production share (~78%) with pack-level CTP/CTC supply share (~62% per BNEF supply chain report). The 78% figure is correct for cells, not for finished CTP/CTC packs because LG Energy Solution and Panasonic also produce pack-level CTP designs. Correction required.

payload.reason: Verification surfaced one material correction (China supply share 78% -> 62%) and one nuance (manufacturer-claimed parts reduction) - analyst must integrate corrections into the final brief, not reproduce the original error

payload.purpose: synthesize verified research into 200-word board-pre-read brief with strategic frame

payload.output:
**EV Battery CTP/CTC Market - Board Pre-Read**

**Players:** CATL leads at 35% global share (Qilin platform), BYD second at 17% (vertically integrated, not merchant), LG Energy Solution 13%, Panasonic 6%, Samsung SDI 5%. The market is consolidating.

**Cost:** Pack-level cost has fallen from $115/kWh (2022) to $82/kWh (2025), with a clear trajectory toward $70-72/kWh by 2027 per BNEF. The $100/kWh barrier - long held as the threshold for EV TCO parity with ICE - was decisively breached in 2024.

**Tech direction:** Structural battery (cell-to-chassis) is the 2026-2028 frontier. Tesla, BYD, and Leapmotor have shipping products. Manufacturers claim 30-40% parts reduction, though this is vendor-asserted rather than independently measured. CTC reduces unit cost but creates customer lock-in to a single vendor stack and complicates collision repair.

**Geopolitical risk:** China supplies ~78% of cells globally and ~62% of finished CTP/CTC packs. The U.S. Inflation Reduction Act Section 30D and the EU Critical Raw Materials Act both restrict China-origin content for compliance, creating real near-term pressure on non-China supply build-out (LGES, Panasonic, Samsung SDI).

*Source-verified by independent peer-review pass; one figure corrected from initial draft.*

payload.resolution: Board-quality 206-word executive brief delivered after research, peer-review verification (1 material correction), and synthesis. Demonstrates the value of fact-verifier in catching the cell-share-vs-pack-share conflation that would have shipped to leadership unchecked.
